It belongs to the channel dragnet-development.
There are no code symbols present.
standouts: It references a target using a board:// URI scheme.
It is a minimal, timestamped provenance record linking an attachment to a board item.
The attachment type is explicitly board_items.
conventions: Attachment id uses an att- prefix.
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit timezone offset.
Channel-memory attachments are stored under channel_attachments/.
Target identifiers use a URI-style scheme.
concerns: The file has no payload or descriptive content beyond metadata.
The meaning and content of the referenced board item are not present locally.
The semantics and resolvability of the board:// URI scheme are not explained in this file.
notes: A timestamped stub that records attachment att-78798afc as linked to board item item-7670d97a in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What actual content or artifact does this attachment represent?

purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to serve as an attachment-to-board-item linkage record, and it does exactly that via its metadata fields.
intention_signals: It seems designed to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel attachment and a specific board item so the relationship can be retrieved, audited, or fused later.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as the target identifier in an otherwise conventional attachment metadata stub.
contribution: It contributes a precise, dated attachment-to-board-item reference that a fused system can use to correlate channel attachments with board entries without ambiguity.
